goal: Show that a user can claim more rewards than fairly accrued because reward baseline is synchronized too late.
setup_requirements: at least two users for fairness comparison
minimal_state: user A partially withdraws before reward debt sync
attacker_actions: trigger the balance mutation path before reward baseline update
claim rewards after stale state is preserved long enough to affect payout
assertions: attacker claimed reward exceeds expected fair share
post-claim reward accounting no longer reconciles cleanly against expected distribution
common_failures: claim path performs a full synchronization before payout
withdraw path settles pending rewards before mutating balance
the suspected path is unreachable due to earlier guard logic
notes: When possible, compare attacker reward against a reference implementation or a manually computed fair-share baseline.
